clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
Diarrhea, Fatigue
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Manual vacuum aspiration to remove remnant fetal tissue

History of Present Illness:
Ms. ___ is a ___ G5P2 who is pregnant ___ with a PMHx 
notable for T1DM who is being admitted to the FICU for acidosis 
and DKA. 

Patient was recently admitted to ___ ICU 
(___) for a UTI and DKA. She was apparently treated with 
antibiotics (unclear if cipro or ceftriaxone). She now reports a 
significant amount of foul smelling diarrhea. Saw OB here this 
week and ended up having a positive CDIFF on ___. Was 
written for Flagyl and filled the Rx on ___ but unclear if she 
has been taking this.

With regard to her T1DM, her most recent HgbA1c was 12.4%. She 
is managed by Dr. ___ at ___. Reports fasting 
around 100, post meal around 150-180.

Patient had a "FIRST-AID" called while she was on the ___ 
___. She was tachy to 120 with BP in the ___ with a FSBG 
>400. She was sent to ED for further evaluation. 

In the ED, initial vitals:  97.6 117 129/71 16 100% RA 
- Labs were notable for: WBC 13.5, Cr 1.3, K 6.1, HCO3 6, pH 
7.0, lactate 3.1, AG 31
- Imaging showed: CXR without PNA
- Was given: insulin gtt, a total of 6.5L NS, K repletion, 
started on D5NS once FSG <200 
- Ob/Gyn was notified
- After above measures, pH improved to 7.26, FSBG to 170s, and 
AG closed 
 
On arrival to
[... truncated ...]

Past Medical History:
T1DM (diagnosed ___

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY: I&D of thigh abscesses ___ and 
___

PAST OBSTETRIC HISTORY: G4P2, now G5P2
- G1: ___ SVD @ term (___), 6#5oz
- G2: ___ SVD, IUFD @ 33 weeks (___), 3#8oz
- G3: ___ SAB at 6 weeks, Mt ___
- G4: ___, SVD @ 35 wks, IOL for poor glycemic control

PAST GYNECOLOGIC HISTORY: denies abnormal Pap, fibroids, 
gynecologic surgery, STIs
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
No family history of diabetes

Hospital Course:
___ with somewhat poorly controlled DM1, prior complicated 
pregnancy related to same, recent admit at ___ for DKA in 
setting of UTI, who presented with DKA at 6 weeks gestation and 
was found to have C difficile colitis along with unfortunately 
missed abortion. 

She was treated for her DKA with insulin GTT and then subQ 
insulin along with IV fluids.

In workup for her diarrhea and DKA she was found to have C diff, 
and she was started on vancomycin with improvement in diarrhea.

She was found to have had a missed abortion, and underwent 
bedside manual vacuum aspiration to remove  remaining fetal 
tissue. 

# DM1 with DKA: Likely precipitated by recent change in insulin 
regimen as well diarrhea and dehydration from C diff infection.
- ___ consulted and made recs while here
- Lantus 20U HS
- Humalog sliding scale

# Abdominal pain after
# Missed abortion requiring D&C
- Tylenol, ibuprofen PRN

# Diarrhea caused by
# C difficile colitis: Improving but still with some diarrhea at 
discharge.
- Continue vancomycin 125mg q6h for 10 more days

# Dysuria: Likely secondary to recent GU procedure. Resolved by 
discharge. 

Disposition: Home without services
Code status: Full code
Billing: >30 minutes spent coordinating discharge home
 
Medications on Admission:
The Preadmission Medication list is accurate and complete.
1. detemir 20 Units Bedtime
Insulin SC Sliding Scale using HUM Insulin
2. Prenatal Vitamins 1 TAB PO DAILY 

 
Discharge Medications:
1. Prenatal Vitamins 1 TAB P
[... truncated ...]

Diabetes type I with ketoacidosis
C difficile infection

PMH: 
- T1DM: dx at age ___, multiple episodes of DKA in the past. 
Usual
endocrinologist is ___ at ___

___: D&C
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
No family history of diabetes

Hospital Course:
___  G6P2(1) admitted at 9 weeks gestataion with poorly 
controlled T1DM. On admission, she was afebrile and without any 
evidence of infection or DKA. ___ was consulted and followed 
her closely. Her HbA1C was quite elevated (12.5%) and Ms ___ 
was counseled regarding the possible implications to her 
pregnancy with poorly controlled diabetes. She was initially 
started on Macrobid for possible UTI and penicillin for dental 
infection. Her urine culture returned negative. In regards to 
her tooth pain, a panorex film and dental consult were obtained. 
There was significant decay in 4 teeth and the patient desired 
extractions. OMFS was consulted and the decision was made to 
defer extraction until the second trimester. This was arranged 
at ___. The penicillin was discontinued given there was no acute 
infection.
.
Her fingersticks improved while she was here. At the time of 
discharge, her insulin regimen was Levemir 16 units in the 
morning, and Humalog ___ with meals. She will have close 
follow up with ___ as an outpatient.
.
In regards with her routine care, her prenatal labs were drawn 
while she was here. A baseline 24 hour urine (414mg) and 
preeclampsia labs were done. She was started on a baby aspirin.
.
Ms ___ complained of a genital lesion which was consistent 
with genital HSV. She was treated with a course of acyclovir.
 
Medications on Admission:
Lantus 30 units QHS, Humalog 10 units + SS at meals, PNV, 
Macrobid ___ BID, PCN 500mg QID
 
Discharge Medications:
[... truncated ...]

clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
nausea, vomiting, dizziness

 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
PICC line placement and removal

History of Present Illness:
Ms. ___ is a ___ year old G6P3 with a history of
T1DM (diagnosed age ___ at ___ who presents with two days of
nausea and vomiting and dizziness. She has been experiencing
"morning sickness" for the past week or two of her pregnancy 
that
makes it difficult to keep meals down throughout the day. For 
the
past two days she has not eaten much at all. Yester day all she
ate was a plum and some juice. As a result, she was not checking
her blood sugars or taking mealtime insulin. She has only taken
half of her evening long acting insulin over the past 2 nights
because she was concerned she might drop low due to not eating.
Her usual regimen is 10 units humolog (sliding scale increase by
1 unit for 50 in glucose), bedtime levemir 18 U. She endorses
polyuria. 

She endorses feeling ill lately, with fevers/chills/nightsweats
for the past few days. She complains of stomach/epigastric pain
for the past two days and pressure-like frontal headace at a 
___
relieved by Tylenol.  She also complains of lower abdominal
pain/cramping for the past week, right side greater that left,
worse when she is walking and improved with rest, not affected 
by
BM. Last BM ___ was normal; denies constipation. She

Hospital Course:
Ms. ___ was admitted to the ___ service after 
presenting with hyperglycemia with nausea and vomiting 
concerning for diabetic ketoacidosis. She had an anion gap 
acidosis which was corrected with fluid hydration and 
administration of insulin and she experienced improvement in her 
symptoms with blood sugar correction. She underwent ___ line 
placement for IV access on the day of admission. 

___ was consulted for assistance with managing her possible 
DKA and her insulin regimen.

On hospital day 3, she continued to have labile blood sugars but 
requested discharge due to family needs. She was counseled 
regarding the recommendation for continued inpatient management 
and she chose to leave against medical advice with plan for 
close outpatient followup. 
 
Medications on Admission:
Insulin humolog 10 U sliding scale; Insulin Levemir 18 U qHS
-Prenatal vitamin 
-Aspirin 81 mg PO daily

 
Discharge Medications:
Levimir 14u bedtine
Humalog 5 breakfast, 3 lung, 4 dinner
PNV

Home

___ ___ at ___ transferred from ___
with concern for DKA. 

She states she had 6 teeth extracted on ___. She had limited PO
intake due to pain and discomfort. She has been taking Tylenol.
She took a course of penicillin prophylactically prior to her
procedure. She did not take her home dose of Humalog given she
was not eating. However, she still took Levemir nightly. 

On ___, she was walking outside and was struck by a car on her
left side. She denies abdominal trauma or falling on her 
abdomen.
She went home afterwards to rest and continue Tylenol. She then
woke up ___ with vaginal spotting. Denies contractions, loss of
fluid. +FM. She presented to ___ due to the
spotting. She had a speculum exam which showed a closed cervix.
Her spotting resolved with no further episodes of bleeding. 

On evaluation at ___, she was found to have elevated blood
glucose to 504 on Chem-7. She then had a fingerstick of 419. She
was given a dose of Humalog (unknown dosage) and transferred to
___ for further evaluation. 

Here, she states she still has left sided pain from her 
accident.
She is feeling the baby moving well. She denies episodes of
hypoglycemia or hyperglycemia. S

Hospital Course:
Ms. ___ is a ___ G6P3 with a h/o T1DM admitted with 
poor glycemic control, also s/p left sided flank impact from car 
accident while walking. She was admitted to the antepartum unit. 
On admission, her anion gap was 16 which down trended to 10. She 
was started on levemir 14 units QHS and Humalog SSI. She was 
also complaining of left flank pain on admission. However, there 
were no clinical signs of abruption and ultrasound (___) was 
reassuring. She underwent fetal testing that was reassuring. 

Patient was advised to remain in the hospital for 24 hours of 
observation, but she elected to leave against medical advice. 
She was counseled on the risks of poor glycemic control and 
preterm labor and given return precautions. She was subsequently 
discharged home against medical advice, with outpatient 
follow-up scheduled. 
 
Medications on Admission:
PNV, aspirin, levemir 14units qhs, Humalog SSI 
 
Discharge Medications:
PNV, aspirin, levemir 14units qhs, Humalog SSI

Home

Type 1 diabetes,

clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
contractions, N/V, HA, decreased fetal movement, general malaise
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none

History of Present Illness:
___ G6P1222 at 30w1d with poorly controlled T1DM, worsening 
proteinuria, elevated BP x1, fetal CNS abnormalities and lagging 
AC with abnormal dopplers, concern for placental insufficiency 
presenting with persistent contractions, nausea and vomiting x2 
days, headache s/p Tylenol without improvement, decreased fetal 
movement and generally feeling unwell. Reports she does not feel
like this is DKA, has had multiple episodes of DKA in the past. 

Patient was recently discharged from the antepartum service on 
___ after not meeting criteria for pre-eclampsia, was since 
then recommended for admission for fetal monitoring in the 
setting of abnormal Dopplers (elevated with present diastolic 
flow) which she declined against medical advice. She is 
scheduled for repeat Dopplers tomorrow. Betamethasone has been 
deferred thus far due to concerns for glycemic control and risk 
of DKA. 

Regarding her T1DM, she is unsure whether she has been taking 14 
vs 20 units of levemir but reports most recently taking 10 units 
of Humalog with lunch around 3pm.  She has been able to keep 
down water and orange juice since developing nausea and vomiting 
yesterday. She reports feeling heartburn. 


[... truncated ...]

PNC: 
- ___ ___ by ultrasound 
- Labs O pos/Abs neg/Rub ___ NR/HBsAg neg/HIV neg/GBS 
unknown

- Issues
*) agenesis of the corpus callosum and dilated ___ ventricle: 
s/p
amnio (___) - 46XY, s/p ___ consult with plan for follow up
___
*) lagging fetal growth with elevated Dopplers
*) T1DM: followed by ___, A1C 11.1% (___) 
*) Poor dentition: s/p 6 teeth extraction ___ 
*) hx IUFD

[... truncated ...]

Hospital Course:
___ G6P3 with T1DM admitted at 30w1d with pre-eclampsia, 
preterm contractions, fetal anomalies and concern for placental 
insufficiency. On admission, she was afebrile and without any 
evidence of infection or preterm labor. She was comfortable and 
her cervix was 1/long/high. Fetal testing was overall 
reassuring. In regards to her T1DM, her fingerstick on arrival 
was 350. She had no evidence of DKA and her glucose values 
improved with SC insulin. She was noted to have mild range blood 
pressures on arrival which ruled her in for preeclampsia given 
her worsening proteinuria and elevated BP during prior 
admission. Preeclampsia labs were normal. A course of 
betamethasone was administered (complete ___. ___ was 
consulted and followed her closely. She was monitored with twice 
daily NSTs which were reassuring. Her blood pressures remained 
mostly normal on the floor. She remained clinically stable until 
30w6d when she left the hospital AMA despite the strong 
recommendation to stay inpatient. A plan was made for daily 
fetal testing at both ___ and ___.
 
Medications on Admission:
PNV, aspirin, levemir 14units qhs, Humalog 10u with meals 
 
Discharge Medications:
Humalog 17 Units Breakfast
Humalog 14 Units Lunch
Humalog 17 Units Dinner
levemir 6 Units Breakfast
levemir 16 Units Bedtime
Insulin SC Sliding Scale using HUM Insulin 
Prenatal Vitamins 1 TAB PO DAILY
 
Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis (prior stay — from no
